ntradiction here is that the king's first house has a width of
108 cubits. If its height were to be 108 cubits, it would be violating
the rule of maximum height,] These half-baked scholars, he conti-
nues, explain away this difficulty by referring to one as the general
rule and to the other as the exception. In fart there is neither in
this case. For, in verse 22 our author has given the height of the
first floor as 10 cubits 18 digits and of the next floor (storey) as the
above reduced by a twelfth.
Visvakarman has laid down that mighty emperors could have a
mansion of 71 storeys; Brahmanas, 6i; Ksatriyas 5^; Vaisyas4j; and
Sudras 3? storeys :
